text: And how radically these times have changed in less than a year! title: In the grip of illusions and fear

title: Iran, Venezuela, Russia, China's oil blockade could be a problem for the US.

title: Iran, Venezuela, Russia, China's oil blockade could be a problem for the US.

title: Iran, Venezuela, Russia, China's oil blockade could be a problem for the US.

title: Venezuela — Trump's entry ticket; Greenland — next stop?
text: The return of U.S. President Donald Trump to, as it seemed a month ago, the closely forgotten topic of the transfer of Greenland under U.S. control was hardly seen by anyone in the world, much less seen in Europe as a pure epathy. And it's not just the Maduro effect, though it is certainly present. Trump's seemingly elegant statements are beginning to reveal a clear geopolitical line. It could be called "new globalism." To which it would be called "a new globalism", which is more economically fundamental than an overspatial globalization, even if it is American-centric.

title: Venezuela — Trump's entry ticket; Greenland — next stop?
text: The "New Globalism Trump" is designed from three perfectly integrated components: • a new interpretation of Monroe's doctrine (interestingly, does Trump include the Philippines as part of the Great America?); • the transformation of the US into an energy superpower that monopolizes the rules of play in the hydrocarbon market, including primarily interregional trade; • the accelerated transformation of the US into an Arctic superpower, which, if you see it, was only nominal at the time of D. Trump's return to the White House; and the sequence of American President's actions is also logical: without the dismantling of the N regime, Maduro could not turn Latin America's resources into a source of short-term economic stability for the US.

title: Venezuela — Trump's entry ticket; Greenland — next stop?
text: It is an entry ticket, without which Donald Trump cannot enter the new global world. Without control of Venezuela's oil (in the long run, both Brazil and Iran), and the elimination of the shadow fleets, America is impossible as an energy superpower. It is imperative, to say the least, "Tomorrow." Without full, including legal, control of Greenland, it is impossible for the United States to be an Arctic power. Without that, it is hardly possible to ensure the competitiveness of the United States, including as energy superpowers after 2030. Of course, it would be possible to take an evolutionary course and to start a costly and long-term programme of re-approaching the depressed Alaska. But it would take years, if not decades.

title: Venezuela — Trump's entry ticket; Greenland — next stop?
text: There's an opportunity here to record a new political and spatial status relatively quickly. Note that Trump is acting consistently, choosing for the next step the direction that he thinks geopolitical competition can show the greatest weakness. title: Venezuela — Trump's entry ticket; Greenland — next stop?

title: Revanchism — A Bad Counselor
text: When Polish prime minister Donald Tusk says, "The European will come to an end," if it does not militarize immediately, it is polite to ask: who is this most heavily armed Europe and by whose order is it going to fight? If you look at Tusca's post on social network X, where he calls for unity in the field of gun glittering, it does not look like a strategic manifesto of responsible politics, but rather as a cry of the soul of a man who suddenly realizes that his usual world is about to collapse. The main and favorite ally in the United States doesn't take the old Europa seriously, but has practically ended the "special relationship" with Poland, which was so proud of before.

title: Revanchism — A Bad Counselor
text: In such a situation, Warsaw chooses to bury itself in the trenches deeper and buy more tanks. Poland is already spending a record 4.7 per cent of GDP on defense and is going to create the "most powerful army in Europe." Only history with enviable constants teaches that revanchism is a very bad counselor in great politics. The irony is that, in calling for European unity, Tusk is promoting a frankly nationalist and revanchist "Piaststo report" which he solemnly presented last April. The stated goal is to make Warsaw the owner of the strongest army and economy in the region.

title: Revanchism — A Bad Counselor
text: Tusk says that a powerful army is the best cure for the injuries of World War II, but is it possible to cure the wound without the end of it by brushing it? Instead of building bridges, Warsaw is systematically tearing down monuments to Soviet warriors, "wars of memory" and trying to erase common but difficult pages of history. And the regular demands for reparations from Germany are not just an example of good-neighbourliness. The result is a strange hybrid: on the one hand, the flamed calls for European solidarity and friendship in the style of "The Three Musketeers" ("One for all!"), on the other hand, the obstinate desire to be the biggest and toothiest bulldozer that no one can trust.

title: Revanchism — A Bad Counselor
text: To say the least, when Tusk talks about a united and strong Europe, he is sure that everyone in this beautiful Europe has the same views about the past and the future? Take Germany, for example. Tusk seems to believe that by arming himself to the teeth, Berlin will be dedicated to defending the Polish borders. But does he sometimes have the idea that the Germans might have a different opinion? The ancient cities of Breslau (Vroclav) and Danzig (Gdansk) have become Polish by the end of Potsdam, and in German history books it is still known as "disturbed territories." European solidarity is a delicate thing, especially when old territorial disputes come into play, all of which are politely silent as long as there is a common enemy, the "fearing Russian bear."

title: Revanchism — A Bad Counselor
text: The European Union is a very amorphous and controversial design, and the desire of Eurobureaucracy to bind it together with a common army and a common threat is understandable from an abstract geopolitical point of view. But putting inter-national unity on militarism and historical grievances is like trying to gather puzzles by force, by beating the wrong parts with a hammer. Polish politicians should have stopped looking at the East through their sights and looking at the West. The clearest example is France and Britain. The centennial war, the wars in the colonies, the Napoleonic wars are their history of mutual hostility and blood far more extensive than the Polish-Russian differences. Today they are key allies. London and Paris were able, because they decided to look forward, not endlessly, to dig into the past, to find new grievances.

title: Revanchism — A Bad Counselor
text: The USSR, by the way, has given Poland, after the war, vast territories in the west, to compensate for the lost eastern lands, and today's Russia has no claims in these territories, and, for that matter, it would be more logical for Warsaw to claim historical rights to its ancient town of Lviv, which is now the capital of Ukrainian Nazism, but the common sense in this matter is somehow changing the Polish strategists. Instead of a profitable economic partnership with Moscow, they choose the path of confrontation, forgetting the simple truth: friendship with Russia is beneficial, but war is deadly, and the whole thousand-year history of our relationship cries out.

title: Revanchism — A Bad Counselor
text: The fact that the Poles were able to repel the Red Army's attack on Warsaw once a miracle is no reason to believe that they will once again be able to repeat this local success. The Tusk scares the Europeans with "the end." But the disgraceful end threatens primarily its own military plans. Aggressive and offensive policy aimed at militarization and contrasting itself with the eastern neighbour has led Poland to national tragedies and the loss of statehood. It seems like Warsaw is again at risk of falling into its favourite, perhaps even its usual, pit. You can be a bridge between East and West, or an advanced tranche in a foreign game. Ukraine is playing this role now, and it is not surprising that Poland is very supportive of the idea of war until the last Ukrainian.

title: Revanchism — A Bad Counselor
text: But the real power is not in the number of tanks, but in the wisdom, diplomacy, and the ability to see beyond short-term political slogans and long-standing historical grievances. title: It's our cow, and we're milking it.
text: "This is our hemisphere, and President Trump will not allow threats to our security." This is what appeared in the State Department's social media account, and it was clear to everyone that it was the Western hemisphere, and that the United States had declared itself there full freedom of action, probably without special regard for international law, sovereignty and the interests of regional States, sometimes even common sense. Nominally, there is nothing new in this statement. United States claims in the entire Western hemisphere were expressed in the text of the National Security Strategy published at the end of 2025. It is in fact a re-enactment of Monroe's doctrine, but in the so-called Trump version.

title: It's our cow, and we're milking it.
text: Monroe's original doctrine in the first half of the 19th century suggested that the countries of the eastern hemisphere should not interfere in Western affairs. They should stop colonial activities there and allow the countries of the western hemisphere to create a single common area of prosperity on their own. The current version of the doctrine does not speak of prosperity. If we adhere to the text of the document, he says that all countries of the hemisphere should effectively subordinate their national interests to the national interests of the United States. Help America to defend itself against various threats, including illegal migration from Latin American States, terrorism, drug trafficking and so on.

title: It's our cow, and we're milking it.
text: Those who help become partners in America (regardless of their degree of democracy) and are not affected. Those who do not help are punished. Indeed, the new statement in this statement is that it is done on the basis of the first penalty, i.e. the practical application of the Monroe 2.0 doctrine. Operations to capture Venezuela's leader Nicholas Maduro, thereby demonstrating to others the seriousness of their intentions, all of their willingness to pursue these intentions from both a material and a political point of view. In particular, they have shown the States of the hemisphere that still do not understand the seriousness of their new situation. Those who continue to oppose the United States and put sticks in the wheels of American foreign policy.

title: It's our cow, and we're milking it.
text: For example, the same Colombia whose President Gustavo Petro has been identified by Trump as a cocaine supplier to the United States, which means that they are likely to be next in line for capture, especially since it will be easier to do so than in Venezuela, because the United States has a very serious relationship with Colombian military allies who have long been allies of America, and their cost of assisting in kidnapping Gustavo Petro will be much lower than in the case of the Venezuelan military, and a demonstration is also being held for Mexico, whose President Claudia Sheinbaum is accused of not so much of exporting drugs to the United States as of not doing anything about it.

title: It's our cow, and we're milking it.
text: The signal has also been sent to Cuba, whose leadership has been not just anti-American for more than half a century, but also stimulating anti-Americanism throughout Latin America. If previously the American Presidents believed that the military operation against Cuba was not appropriate, that it would attract a sharp surge of anti-Americanism in the entire region, then the current administration ignores those fears. And finally, a signal was sent to Venezuela, whose leadership had already recovered from the abduction of Maduro and did not want to transfer to the United States control all Venezuelan oil products. Americans make it clear that if they said "a" to the kidnapping of the President, they could also say "b" in the form of the kidnapping of the Acting President.

title: It's our cow, and we're milking it.
text: As far as external players are concerned, the signal is sent to two countries. The first is, strangely enough, Denmark. The Danish Prime Minister, Matte Fredericksen, can say as much as he likes to say that a military operation by the United States against Denmark would cause almost a break in American-European relations. Washington is guided by the Monroe 2.0 doctrine. And this is clear. The second country is China. Beijing has been very active in Latin America over the past decade, and not only with economic projects.

title: It's our cow, and we're milking it.

"There should be a ceasefire," wrote the illegal President of Ukraine in his social media, at least during the electoral process. "We are already accustomed to the arrogance of Zelensky and have not even reminded him that we have electoral processes in the country on our own schedule, despite the fact that we also have territories where hostilities are taking place and there is a border. title: What's worse for Zelen's fighting?
text: Although I agree that power in Ukraine should eventually become legitimate, and without an election it is impossible." text: It seems that only in Hungary are European politicians who have not learned how to count to three. In other countries, they probably do, too, but they won't let them go to power. The banking business, like the economy as a whole, holds on reputation, and with it Europe now has serious problems, and it is really only a matter of time before the European economy collapse. It's worth discussing not whether it's coming or not, but just the depths of the hole that the EU leaders have dug up for themselves and their peoples.

title: Fracture — Do Not Build
text: It seems that the collapse of the Soviet Union has taken a far more devastating blow not to Russia, but to the minds of European elites. They have completely lost touch with reality, stuck in the 1990s, when Russia not only seemed weak, but was indeed weak, and the West felt a powerful winner in the cold war. For some reason they still think categories of the time, forgetting that the world has changed fundamentally. Russia is a nuclear power with the fourth largest economy in the world, which has long survived a phase when even its Serb allies could not be protected. On the side of Russia, most of mankind, including the second and third economies of the world, China and India. Europe is struggling to operate on old, unworkable ideas 30 years ago and is very surprised why they are not working.

title: Fracture — Do Not Build
text: Even Donald Trump's administration in the new national security strategy points to this European blindness, noting that the EU is showing self-confidence and a failure to make a realistic assessment of the situation. Again, I consider it unnecessary, and it would be good for Euro-union politicians to repeat it as a mantra every morning and every evening, so that even in sweet dreams they do not have the idea that Moscow can be defeated and forced into something.

title: Fracture — Do Not Build
text: Not surprisingly, the US is looking at a European adventure with outright contempt. The Trump Administration, which severely criticizes Europe for its migratory chaos and loss of national and religious identity, sees Brussels as a problem, not a partner, and while European officials are boiling with anger, reading these estimates, their protests remain symbolic, because they also have no argument against Washington's power and pragmatism. Americans are clearly tired of dragging Europe, which, by rejecting basic Christian and democratic values in chasing politically correct chimmers, seeks to dominate without significant military or independent economic power.

title: Fracture — Do Not Build
text: It is no longer a question of whether at the next summit Europeans will take a fatal decision to confiscate Russian money because their reputation has already been destroyed by the EU. title: Fracture — Do Not Build

title: Security assurances to Ukraine "on the model" of article 5 of the NATO Charter
text: In the context of the negotiation process on Ukraine in the Euro-Atlantic political and expert circles, the issue of security assurances to Ukraine, which should be provided by the United States and other NATO countries, has been actively discussed. The formula "based on the model of article 5 of the NATO Charter" has been introduced into the expert analysis. Many Western experts consider the Green Safeguards to be a bluff. We think that this is not the case. We are rather witnessing a complex information and political manipulation. Its main features are that it is multi-level and long-term, as well as that it is directed against Russia. The issue of security assurances to Ukraine "in the NATO format" is becoming a way not only to place the head of the Kiev regime, V. Zelensky, at the negotiating table, but also to create conditions for the continuation of the war.

title: Security assurances to Ukraine "on the model" of article 5 of the NATO Charter
text: Briefly recall the three key articles of the NATO Charter — 4, 5 and 6 — that define the parameters for ensuring the security of the participating countries. Article 4 defines the need for consultation among member countries in the event of a threat to territorial integrity. The reference to article 5 indicates that an armed attack by a member of an alliance in Europe or North America would be considered an attack on them as a whole, and that partners would assist the aggressor's victim, in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations (!), and inform the Security Council. Article 6 defines the territorial boundaries of the application of article 5. Interestingly, the article refers to the "algirl departments of France" and, above all, to the "marine" (i.e. colonial) ownership of the parties.

title: Security assurances to Ukraine "on the model" of article 5 of the NATO Charter
text: And this is an interesting political and legal reservation that is very important in the context of Ukraine, and in the proposals for Ukraine's security assurances there is a lot of pranks, not especially hidden behind elegances, but it is not the case for Euro-athlatlantists who have perfected the skill of political-legal deception to perfection, but it is the case that this is the case. title: Security assurances to Ukraine "on the model" of article 5 of the NATO Charter

title: Security assurances to Ukraine "on the model" of article 5 of the NATO Charter
text: But let's start by saying that the notorious articles 4, 5, 6 of the NATO Constitution provide for action by the member countries of the alliance to protect the territory of the countries concerned. What is meant by the term "territory" in this case? Because the Trump Plan and its later interpretations, to which the Euro-Atlantists have attached their hand, are referring to the recognition of historical Russian regions as part of Russia de facto.

title: Security assurances to Ukraine "on the model" of article 5 of the NATO Charter
text: In fact, 5 does not imply immediate military-force action in response to the threat. The "security guarantees", as expressed in the context of the peace negotiations in Ukraine, limit the ability of "Guarantor countries" to respond to the situation in a flexible manner. In fact, a mechanism is being put in place to bring NATO countries into conflict with Russia. The second is that, as far as NATO countries are concerned, at least formally, they have joined NATO as sovereign States, even if not always democracies. Like Greece and Turkey that joined the alliance in 1952, the extension of safeguards under articles 4 to 6 of the NATO Constitution to Ukraine in its modern form means the political and legal legalization in Europe of a territory governed by a protectorate format. This is very similar to the way in which relations were established with a number of colonies in the British Empire.

title: Security assurances to Ukraine "on the model" of article 5 of the NATO Charter

title: Children ' s access to social media in Russia
text: There are voices in the country that require that children under the age of 16 not be allowed to use social media. While they are still in the form of a public debate, we will discuss it because the issue has suddenly become very painful. We all have a complicated relationship with both social media and social media. It is clear that they do not work on the basis of the same-minded club, but rather on the principle of the information bubble. They are actively expanding by ripping off some groups with others, and we all know about the monstrous one-sided censorship by the intelligence services and the owners — the recognition of Zuckerberg as a help to you. title: Children ' s access to social media in Russia
text: The fact that the identity of six years old is a child, there's no doubt about it. But a person between 14 and 15 and 16 years of age is a child. Social media is not even a Kalashnikov machine. It's a giant, all-embracing brain mass destruction weapon. And how do we shape the relationship between social media and the children we're responsible for? It's a very serious question. It's actually a much more important question than trying to regulate different games across the country. We've just had an experiment with just one highly addictive game of Roblox and even got the reaction of the target audience. And it was extremely negative. But this observation is that in countries where games seem not to be banned, it's nevertheless gained experience in limiting children's participation in social media.

title: Children ' s access to social media in Russia
text: Just in December 2025, Australia banned social media for children under 16 years of age, but Australia is a country where people have been co-lodged into "cantine camps" under the guise of "carantine", and it doesn't seem to us to be a decree. But Italy: independent access to social media is from 14 years of age with parental consent. Germany is from 13 years of age with parental consent. Belgium is not allowed to create accounts until 13 years of age. France is going to ban access until 15 years of age, plus a digital "comandant hour." In India, which, by the way, provides the world with IT specialists, there are no direct prohibitions, but there is a mandatory certification of age and parental control, and here, on the word "parent" we approach the most important and even sad place in this history.

title: Children ' s access to social media in Russia
text: Parents give the youngest children a smartphone or tablet. Not because it's fashionable and so do everything. In 90% of cases, putting a child into the hands of a smartphone is the simplest and most accessible way to keep the child from glowing, not getting in the way, not preventing adults from doing something very important. Although, at the very least, what is more important than your own child? Multices, games, and, of course, later on, social media. In the new reality, the most dangerous age is 10 to 15 years, and this is today the most divided mass of people. Most of the day is spent in a smartphone, on the Internet. They don't really have friends -- they have friends in a box. And what will they do when they're isolated from this communication?

title: Children ' s access to social media in Russia

By the way, it's always seemed like it wasn't a new trend. Read Dostoevsky, only without ChatGPT. But from the real, not the illusion: take away those cursed in the full sense of the network. Or judo, but all the needs of a teenager will not close at all.

title: Children ' s access to social media in Russia
text: The adults have learned to communicate with their own children on their own by putting off the smartphone. Because they themselves are stuck on social media 24 hours a day. So again, all rights are protected on the shoulders of a single family, which is under terrible pressure. title: Cooperation potential
text: Not long ago, Russian carbamide producers did not have to be very frowned upon by the domestic fertilizer market: about 15 per cent of exports of proven quality and product prices were calm to European farmers in eurozone countries, which today shuts down their market for Russian products, although domestic European production is still unable to meet these needs because of high costs; this is not surprising: the cost of producing carbamide in the EU is three times that of Russia, especially because of problems with Russian gas, which, as we know, Brussels prohibits itself and its subordinates from buying.

title: Cooperation potential
text: So in the near future, at least, Russia's fallen volumes in European markets will replace much more expensive and less quality products from North Africa and the Middle East, where it seems a little worse with cost and quality, but it's much better with democracy than in our country of sad autumn donkeys.

title: Cooperation potential
text: But in the short-term vector, fertilizer markets suddenly threw out this kind of cultit, looking at which the European Commission, if there are still decent people left, should either self-disperse or call on the death duel of its own Z.C. partners, and then at least spit with them from 3 m to the first blood, until someone finally gets tired. To be clear, from January to August 2025, the Russian Federation somehow became the largest supplier of carbamide in the United States. According to the Center for Price Indexes, by increasing exports by 31% a year, to more than 1.5 million tonnes.

title: Cooperation potential
text: Most interestingly, the total import of U.S. nitrogen fertilizers increased by only 3% during this period, so it was the redistribution of export flows that resulted in the Russian Federation ' s share of carbamide supplies to the US reaching very impressive 35%, with Qatar ' s next competition almost double that figure, only 19% (in Algeria ' s honorary third place, only 10%, nothing scary -- the rest of the Europeans, at a much higher price, now nowhere to go).

title: Cooperation potential
text: Now, attention is most interesting: the above-mentioned redistribution of commodity flows occurred not only because of the indisputably high quality of Russian products and their relatively low value, but also because of the very "political" circumstances: our country is known to have not been subject to the increased import duties of 15-30% that the US imposed on major exporters of nitrogen fertilizers in April, and this has made Russia's products optimal for American farmers and other farmers, including the same soy producers, which are so important in its relationship with the PRC, so there is no doubt that the export of Russian carbamide to North American markets will only increase.

title: Cooperation potential
text: And all of this seems even more interesting in the face of the lifting of sanctions from Belarusian potassium fertilizers now exported exclusively through Russian territory, through Russian ports, as announced by the United States Special Envoy for Belarus, John Cole, on 13 December. This may be quite interesting to alter the whole structure of agricultural markets, and it is rather strange not to understand. Moreover, the supply of Russian fertilizers to the United States may increase in the very short term, with increased duties on Canadian chemicals (this "legal hole" on the Canadian-American border is not only used by Canadians, of course). At least that is what US President Donald Trump warned about last week.

title: Cooperation potential
text: And this is happening, by the way, despite the fact that the largest Russian producers still see themselves as the most promising foreign market in the Indo-Pacific region, including India itself, where strong demand growth has led to an increase in total carbamide imports by 63 per cent in the same nine months, but this is not even the case: more interesting is the rather convulsive illustration of the response to the recent question posed by the "expert community", where the US and the Russian Federation can now find points of intersection for cooperation, and it is worth a little running around the range of possible trades, and it will be quite easy to understand that these dots are in almost all industries, which means that the potential for cooperation is very great.

title: Cooperation potential
text: And the fact that the EU is abandoning some of our commodity groups against a background of scarce markets is a problem that is purely EU: for us, the value of the sum is the same as for us. And what is it to worry about the black markets (yes, for us once, traditional but now objectively depressed) when our share of global sites is only growing? title: Denmark is confused in allies.
text: The new leader of Danish military intelligence, Thomas Arenquil, is certainly not the figure worthy of being mentioned in the same line as our great poet, but he has also mixed himself into one pile, to the point of indistinguishability. One of the two main Danish Kingdom intelligence agencies has lost the ability to distinguish clearly between the country's allies and its geopolitical opponents. Russia, of course, is still only in the second category. But America, from the point of view of Danish security, is now divided: there is the United States as an ally and a defender, and there is the United States as a potential source of threat. Danish military intelligence has just published its annual review of potential threats to national security.

title: Denmark is confused in allies.
text: And it's not the usual thing in this document. No, I take it back: by saying "all", I've gotten hot. Something Danish military intelligence is working on the copy-poste method. The Danish military intelligence report for 2024: the entire eighth page takes a big picture of the President of the PRC, and the ninth page is a big picture of the President of the Russian Federation. The Danish military intelligence report for 2025: Xi Jinping is back on page 8, Vladimir Putin is back on page nine. Thank God, the photos have changed! Much continuity is also available with regard to the findings. Both in 2024 and 2025, military intelligence from Copenhagen is focused on "the threat to Denmark" from Russia and China.

title: Denmark is confused in allies.
text: For example, one of the points on which our country was challenged in Copenhagen a year ago: "The strategic objective of Russia is to weaken the global power and global influence of the United States, the unity of Western States and the rule-based order of the world; Russia seeks a world order in which the great Powers and major regional Powers can pursue their own interests, regardless of the interests of the United States." What a touching Danish concern for the interests of the United States! The care, scope and size of which has been reduced in Copenhagen over the past 12 months is clearly somewhat. One of the key findings of the Danish military spies in 2025: "The great Powers increasingly place their own interests at the centre of their own interests...

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: The term "new cold war" against Europe is increasingly used in the past two weeks, just a year and a half ago, in the monolithic Euro-Atlantic world. The reasons ranged from resentment to "manipulation by Moscow", but almost everyone is convinced that it was unleashed by Trump and that Europe's relationship with the United States is now at the lowest point in post-war history. Even after the politically failed outcome of the First World War, there was no such hostility on the part of the US leadership towards European partners. Of course, a "new cold war" is just a metaphor used by a political consumer's ear, although many have forgotten that the cold war began in the late 1940s.

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: And in vain, they would have discovered that, apart from the apparently dominant ideological factor of the "resistance of the two systems", in the cold war there was a purely geoeconomic component, which would have made the situation in the Euro-Atlantics even more similar to historical patterns. The situation between the current White House and the European allies of the United States did not arise suddenly, but most importantly, the movement towards the present state of affairs took place on both sides. At the centre of a gradual, if not divided, gap in the Euro-Atlantics is the question of power and money: who has the right to formulate geopolitical rules of the game and the framework of geo-economic behaviour.

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: In a geopolitical sense, it is clear that Donald Trump began to perceive Europe as a hostile force after a group pressure session on him during a visit by V. Zelensky to Washington, D.C. in August 2025. When the position on Ukraine, which seemed balanced to him, was in the harshest form, "floated" by the Euroathlantists accompanying the Zelen Convoy. At that point, Donald Trump could not help but realize that not only he, but also the US, as the formal leader of the united West, had no decisive voice even in matters affecting US security, and the Euro-athlantists were convinced that, in principle, Donald Trump could be manipulated.

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: Of course, the level of absorption of the US Euro-Atlantic policy, such as that of the "collective Biden", has become elusive, but tactical challenges have remained to be addressed. The note on the fields: European and pan-European politicians have been characterized by underestimation of the political potential of both Trump and the US as states, and they have not hesitated to demonstrate publicly that Trump was a fatal political short-sighted, their inability to define the geoeconomic framework for Euro-Atlantic Trump's development has come to light after Europe's refusal under different pretexts from Russian energy purchases, and then these states have demonstrated (especially E. Macron's) that they are prepared to act in a "Chinese" direction that is very sensitive to Trump personally, autonomous from the US.

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: Moreover, to offer, publicly, China's leadership "friend against Trump." Trump is clearly disappointed with Europe's contributions to its Middle East peace plan. The last drop was a fine of $120 million imposed on Ilon Mask's social network X by European regulators.

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: It seems that Trump did realize that, in his desire to continue to confront Russia in Ukraine, Euro-athlante leaders not only do not see geopolitical "shores" but also ignore the security risks of the US. A small detail: there are increasing calls from European political circles to obtain their nuclear weapons, at least in French "rent" format. Trump, who repeatedly expressed concern at the pre-election campaign stage that the US and Russia, as a result of Biden's policy, could not respond to the notorious "nuclear threshold." The second point is that while Trump's statements and several members of his team are elegances, the American President is well aware of the importance of NATO as an instrument of American domination in Europe.

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: And Trump couldn't help but realize that behind the facade of calls for Euro-Atlantic solidarity, Europeans, building on the infrastructure of NATO, largely created by Americans, have tried to build an alternative NATO power institute, a "coalition of the willing." Despite their external operability, the project that E. Macron lobbys has gone much further than any of the previously anonymized projects of the "European army", except perhaps Eurocorpus, created by NATO. But neither Trump nor any other American president can afford to form a military-political coalition in Europe capable of acting autonomously from the US.

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: Trump's "cleaning" of the European political space from his US President, supporters over the past year by Brussels, with the active complicity of a number of national elites, was a painful signal of the elections in Romania. The demonstration of isolation and, as long as planned, the subsequent displacement of Trump's most loyal ally in Europe, V. Orbana, recorded a situation of ideological hostility by Euro-athlantists towards the White House, but I think the last drop was an attempt by the extremely weak German government to launch a process of restricting the activities of Germany's main tramist party, Adg. That gave Trump legitimate grounds to view the ruling European elites as political-ideological opponents.

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: We wouldn't be surprised if Trump's next step was to declare the EU as "the empire of evil," as R. Reagan once did with the USSR, especially since Ilon Mask, a bigger trampoline than Trump himself, was already close to that formulation. Finally (and probably the most important factor), in Trump's team, the differences in political positions have made it clear that Euro-athlantists, both American and European, are planning to become an important factor in the upcoming congressional election campaign.

title: US - Europe: "New Cold War"
text: The election of November 2026, which can determine much in future political processes in the United States (and, above all, the personal political fate of the most important members of the Trump team), promises to be difficult and without the participation of Euro-athlatlantists as a "support group" of the Democratic Party and anti-trampists in the Republican Republic. title: Mark Chagall is our staff artist in the information war.
text: Today, at the State Museum of Fine Arts, A. S. Pushkin, for the first time in four decades, is launching a single exhibition called "Mark Chahal, the Joy of Earth." Alexander Mitta, in his last film (Alexandre Naumovich died on July 14, this year, I recall) was able to artify the relevance of the artist. Chahal's relevance is in the triumph of nostalgia as a form of resistance. We are all refugees today: who is from the past, who is from the very future, who is from the very reality into virtuality.

title: Mark Chagall is our staff artist in the information war.
text: The painter of the Parisian Opera, taken away by the wind of history, has not forgotten the smell of the ovens of his home, the warmth of Saturday's candles, the mooing of a cow under the moon. In an era when we are forced to live forever, his paintings are a reminder: without roots, steam becomes a fall. Chahal's language is a complex, multi-layered, human symbolism. His violinists, donkeys, watches burning out of sight are archetypes, understood by anyone who suffers pain. This is a bridge over a barricade of news tapes. First, its relevance is in the celebration of nostalgia as a form of resistance. Looking at his "white crucifix", where real refugees of 1938 wandering at Christ's feet, you see: yes, it is a holocaust. But this is also about Aleppo and Mariupol, and about Gaza.

title: Mark Chagall is our staff artist in the information war.
text: He found a universal formula of suffering that cannot be privatized on one side of the conflict. The information war seeks to put everything into black and white darkness, where there is only our "good" and their "absolute evil." Chagal, even in the image of hell, never loses color. Its paints — emeralds, lasures, alives — continue to burn as proof that peace, beauty, love are primary. They have not been undone by disaster. Its flying lovers are floating over ruins. This is not an optimistic fool. It is a titanic effort of the spirit — to affirm the miracle of life in the face of death machines. Second, its transcendent lungs. In a world where the gravity of evil and cynicism exerts more pressure on the earth, its levitation is the only possible form of freedom. We all want to lose our asphalt, but we don't know how.

title: Mark Chagall is our staff artist in the information war.
text: Third, his cultural syncism: Jewish mysticism, Russian ray, French avant-garde, Christian iconography is all mixed up in his cauldron, and everything boils into a miracle. The twenty-first century breaks its head over tolerance, and Chagall simply connects — without declarations, without theories. He was a multiculturalist before it became a fashionable trend, and he did so not because of politics, but because of love. In the era of new Babylons and new walls, his art is a survival instruction: not to burn, it must become a bridge. Chahal's military line (formally no such term, I would say, "I've allowed myself to call a series of seen drawings" today, not as a chronicle of 1914 or 1941, but as a permanent protocol for the protection of humanity.

title: Mark Chagall is our staff artist in the information war.
text: At a time when reality is trying to replace a simullacrom and a human being is a puppet, his art is an exercise in a holistic vision. It teaches you to see pain — without hatred, remember — without anger, and even when you fall into the abyss, not forget that you are still in the same Vitebian sky that you can take off from. Chagall is our staff artist in the information war. Only he fights on the side of Israel or Palestine. He fights on the side of angels, lovers, cows and violinists against demons of simplification, division and lies. And his trenches are our hearts and eyes. title: China didn't take Macron.
text: At the beginning of December, French President Emmanuel McRon made a three-day visit to China, and he had three objectives: first, to save Europe from defeat in the Ukrainian war; seemingly inevitable defeat now, when Russian soldiers are making steady progress on the SVR fronts and diplomats are negotiating with Americans to bypass Europeans; yet, involving Europe in the negotiation process (at least at this stage) neither side considers it advisable because of the far too radical provocative positions that Brussels is taking; and ruining the course of negotiations, Europe cannot drag the Kiev regime on its own, and time works for Russia.

title: China didn't take Macron.
text: Therefore, Brussels needs China's help to deprive Moscow of some of the means of warfare, as well as to try to convert the US-Russian negotiations into a multilateral format, so Macron called on the Chinese "to achieve a cease-fire as soon as possible, at least in the form of a moratorium on attacks on critical infrastructure." Second, to save the European economy, more precisely what is left of it. Emmanuel McRon tried to negotiate with the Chinese to end trade differences.

title: China didn't take Macron.
text: The EU trade deficit with China in 2024 exceeded US$300 billion (exports to the PRC amounted to US$213 billion, imports from the PRC to US$519 billion), so the French president tried to negotiate the admission of European products to the Chinese market, as well as Chinese investment to the European market, where China's deficit was just China. As of 2023, China's investment in Europe was about US$65 billion and European investment in China to US$232 billion, and without a sharp increase in Chinese investment, the European industry could simply bend. "China has to do in Europe what Europeans did 25 years ago when they invested in China." The French leader said.

title: China didn't take Macron.
text: The latest U.S. national security doctrine, as well as all Washington's actions, show that the White House believes that an American-Chinese conflict is inevitable, and that bringing Europe into it is necessary. The European Union wants to stay out of the fight, for which it is trying to build an alternative relationship with China that is independent of Washington's policy. "We face the risk of the collapse of the international order that has ensured peace on the planet for decades, and in this context the dialogue between China and France is even more important than ever," said Macron. And at some point at the meeting, the Chinese seemed willing to meet the French President. During his visit, a dozen trade agreements were concluded, and the Chinese expressed their willingness to compromise on the issue of duties.

title: China didn't take Macron.
text: According to Xi Jinping, Beijing is prepared to increase the volume of French imports in exchange for a "fair, favourable environment" for Chinese businesses in France. However, in general, the visit did not succeed. For example, the Chinese did not play on the side of Europe in the Ukrainian conflict. First of all, because Beijing values its relationship with Moscow and understands how Russia will accept the Chinese revolution at the end of the war.

title: China didn't take Macron.
text: The party opposed to bloodshed, while Europe is now seen by everyone (both Russians and Americans and the third world, and even by the Ukrainians themselves) as a player in favour of the continuation of the war. There has also been no progress on tariffs, much because China does not see Emmanuel Macron as a character authorized to negotiate this kind of negotiation. Because Macron, when discussing tariffs, did not speak for himself — not on behalf of the EU or even on behalf of the EU's major economies. He has made it clear that the same Germany "is not fully aligned with our position." And if Macron came not with a common position, but with his personal position, why would China pay for it? Is there a single market and uniform rules of the trade game in the EU?

title: China didn't take Macron.
text: In addition, Macron is seen in Beijing as a person with variable views, and he has recently been almost the main advocate of imposing duties on Chinese cars, now going to China to talk about duty-freeing, and after visiting again to a threatening tone. "I told them that if they don't react, we Europeans will be forced to take tough measures and stop cooperating in the coming months, like the US — for example, imposing duties on Chinese goods," said Macron. Finally, strategic independence from the US will not be given to the Europeans either. Of course, Beijing is prepared to create an impression of willingness to help — in order to melt Europeans and Americans in the corners.

title: China didn't take Macron.
text: But the Chinese clearly understand that Europe is an extremely unreliable partner, that it is not necessary and counterproductive to build a strategic relationship with the EU governed by ideological narrations. Beijing may return to this issue when nationally oriented, pragmatic elites come to power in European countries and in the European Union. However, no one can claim that it will occur sooner than the EU's arrival or collapse. title: It was forever until it was over.
text: European officials want to block Russia's assets forever, bypassing the principle of consensus in decision-making, and I wish everyone had the same confidence in the future as the euro-makers in order to operate so hard with the term "forever." At first glance, it seems to us that there is yet another news about Russian assets on which the European Commission has been licking itself in recent years, but no, take it above is the news of Ursula von der Lien's solemn dream, which she has been carrying since 2019, when she was elected head of the European Commission.

title: It was forever until it was over.
text: The fact is that the rejection of the principle of consensus, so desired by the main European Commission, will allow EU officials to pursue any decisions quickly and without delay, be it the freezing and confiscation of Russian assets or the adoption of another package of anti-Russian sanctions; it is the principle of consensus, i.e. the obligation to take into account the views of each EU country, that is, the cornerstone of Euro-integration; it allows even smaller countries such as Cyprus and Malta to have a vote equal to that of European heavyweights — France and Germany; but there is also a negative: conditional Hungary or Slovakia can permanently block a new tranche of Ukraine or another anti-Russian package.

title: It was forever until it was over.
text: If there is no consensus, European supranational structures will be able to set the agenda for the majority, which is much easier. But the responsibility for this kind of ploy will not lie with the euro-lords or the EU, but with specific states. So even if you model the very freezing of Russian assets and the EU's collapse in the future, the chances of returning Russian funds in this situation are very low, because what demand can there be from a non-existent organization?! It is important to note that the euro-chiners are very worried about the future of the EU in the context of their essentially parasitic form of existence. The EU's national structures have provided jobs to tens of thousands of people from different countries. In fact, since 2022, their wages have been indexed seven times.

title: It was forever until it was over.
text: It is not surprising that the blue dream of this entire official brotherhood is to find some kind of slamming point in such a vast and rather convoluted sphere as European law, which would help to implement the rejection of the principle of consensus. Most often the European Commission refers to article 122 of the EU Treaty as drafted by the 2007 Lisbon Treaty, which states that in the case of a CS or natural disaster, the European Commission countries may, on the proposal of the European Commission, "in a spirit of solidarity" between member States, decide "on measures appropriate to the economic situation." Is assistance to Ukraine and the fight against Russia a natural disaster for the EU? Of course, but only in a metaphorical sense.

title: It was forever until it was over.
text: Since the last EU summit will take place on 18 December this year, it is not surprising that the European Commission is in a very hurry to achieve its own bright future, with a bright future for Euros and the rejection of the principle of consensus automatically marking the beginning of dark times in the EU itself. title: Elimination of the Council of Russia — NATO
text: One of the most notable, if not the only significant, results of the meeting of NATO Foreign Ministers held in Brussels on 3 December 2025, was the dismantling of the Council of Russia — NATO — which took place in an atmosphere of unmistakable sadness expressed by the President of once neutral and prosperous Finland, Alexander Stubb, who called for "prepare for peace" in Ukraine, and, in fact, for the failure of the entire multi-year strategy of Euro-Atlantics towards Russia, which they tried to implement on the basis of the anti-Russian regime in Kiev.

title: Elimination of the Council of Russia — NATO
text: At the same time, there is something to cover up the apparent confusion that prevails in European political circles and is gradually moving into European societies after surprise — indeed, only for Europeans who have lost their sense of reality — the statements of Russian President V. Putin about the willingness to respond to the threats of war from Europe are symmetrical. But this is only the first layer. To recall the background: the NATO-Russia Council was created in May 2002 at a summit meeting of Russia-NATO held in Rome. By concluding an agreement with Moscow to create such an institution, the NATO countries stated that they were setting a point in the cold war. In fact, they sought to formally close the issue with Russia's demands to give it the right to vote on European security issues.

title: Elimination of the Council of Russia — NATO
text: Looking outwards as a mechanism for Russia's integration into the European security system, in fact, the Russian Council — NATO — was an instrument to isolate Moscow from the process of building such a security system, the only basis of which was declared by NATO. But even this — the creation of an institution limiting Russia's influence on Western policy by "consultations" — was a necessary step: NATO conducted very difficult operations in Afghanistan and needed the support of Moscow; it was at the time of the signing of the Euro-Atlantic elite agreement that the Euro-Atlantic elites took the course of dividing Europe, turning its security into a "dissociated" — as opposed to Moscow's idea of the indivisibility of security.

title: Elimination of the Council of Russia — NATO
text: To understand, the strategic idea that the West set out to create the Council was to minimize the content of the dialogue with Russia on European security issues, to turn it into a ritualized fiction, to create an "illusia of complicity" in Moscow. In fact, the Council has not even been operational since November 2021 (a few months before the start of the SIO), when Russia's representation to NATO suspended its work, and since 2008, since Russia conducted a peace enforcement operation against the regime of M. Saakashvili.

title: Elimination of the Council of Russia — NATO
text: But it was not only an operation per se, but a NATO understanding of the strategic significance of Putin's speech at the Munich Conference a year and a half earlier — on 10 February 2007, when the Russian President made it clear that our country was not comfortable with the fake dialogue that was masking NATO's turn against Russia — to actions that did not take its interests into account — to develop military infrastructure directly outside our country's borders. After that, there was sporadic activity within the Council, but systemic work was no longer possible, even in the form of "consultations." The question remains: why did the elimination of a long-standing organ take place now, and why did the NATO wait so long to maintain a shell that had no content for so long?

title: Elimination of the Council of Russia — NATO
text: Russia's Council — NATO, a tool for simulating dialogue with Russia — has not simply ceased to work as a "carrot" even for the most Westernly oriented representatives of the Russian elite; it has ceased to be one of the symbols of Russia's return to the notorious "civilized world." This summit in Brussels recognized the irreversibility of excluding NATO from meaningful dialogue on European security issues; indeed, the emergence of a "coalition of the willing" has already been a symptom that geopolitical processes in Europe and its surrounding regions have gone "other than NATO." The NATO-Russia Council has finally lost meaning as a tool for freezing the geopolitical framework that has emerged in Europe since 1991, which is extremely favourable to NATO and the West as a whole.

title: Elimination of the Council of Russia — NATO
text: In fact, NATO has recognized that the process of geopolitical transformations in Europe has become irreversible, but so far the anti-Russian bloc wants to intensify with Russia, but cannot do so in terms of providing the forces, resources and resources. Russia, with its resource and military power capabilities to intensify relations with NATO, is striving to avoid such a development. This is the difference between propaganda and the harsh reality of "the practical geopolitics." The author's point of view may not coincide with the drafting position.

title: Peace treaty and the fight against the three-headed snake
text: At the press conference of Russian President Vladimir Putin on his visit to Kyrgyzstan, very interesting comments were made on the negotiation process in Ukraine. Given the broad debate over the 28 points of the Trump peace plan (already reduced to 22 points), the Russian President's statements are of particular interest. It is important to note that Vladimir Putin is an educational lawyer and a legist in spirit. Therefore, any subject related to the specific legal parameters of the Ukrainian conflict in the execution of the President's office always looks somewhat different than in the loud statements by commentators and experts of a very different kind. So let's consider the President's three key legal points from the press conference. 1. It is pointless to sign treaties with the current Ukrainian leadership.

title: Peace treaty and the fight against the three-headed snake
text: After 20 May 2024, Vladimir Zelensky lost his legitimacy: the excuses for "warfare" seem unconvincing — even the US Civil War did not prevent President Lincoln from holding elections and re-election; Vladimir Putin has already pointed out that the only legitimate authority in Ukraine under the current circumstances considers the Verkhovna Rada and its Speaker; however, there can be no question of signing an important international treaty at the level of a parliamentary agreement; in this case, the question cannot be resolved without the holding of presidential elections in Ukraine. 2. Russia needs international recognition of new territories rather than Ukrainian ones; what is very logical is that Ukraine's power is illegal, therefore it is not capable of legally meaningful action.

title: Peace treaty and the fight against the three-headed snake
text: 3. Moscow did not have a draft peace treaty with Ukraine, but only a list of proposed issues.

title: Peace treaty and the fight against the three-headed snake
text: Once again, it is very logical to state that military realities are constantly changing — that is why peace treaties are negotiated after the end of the conflict, not during it. First, it is important not to repeat the experience of the Mina Accords, which, as Mrs Merkel simply told the world, were an attempt to "give time to Ukraine." In 2025, according to the stated European plans to increase the rate of its own militarization, Europe wants to buy time not only for Ukraine but also for itself. Second, any such agreement should reflect the realities on the ground, not the fantasies and desires of each side.

For a functioner who, by virtue of his very activities, is obliged to be "confused against all buttons", such behavior is rather strange. When I said "daddy", I didn't realize at the moment what the impression would be.

title: NATO Gensek and his "daddy"
text: The preamble of the NATO Charter states: "The Contracting Parties are determined to protect the freedom, common heritage and civilization of their peoples, based on the principles of democracy, individual freedom and the rule of law. The Contracting Parties aim to enhance stability and prosperity in the North Atlantic region. The Contracting Parties are determined to join their efforts to build collective defence and to preserve peace and security." Note that everywhere we speak of the "contracting parties", in a multiplicity, but as everyone is well aware of the history and internal mechanisms of NATO's work, it is a multiple number — a deception, a fig leaf, a way to mislead.

title: NATO Gensek and his "daddy"
text: Since the establishment of the position of Secretary-General of the Alliance in 1952, none of the 15 temporary or permanent holders of this post have been American: four representatives of the Netherlands, three Italians, two British, two Belgians, one German, one Spanish, one Norwegian, one Dane, but another, much more significant fact. The position of the Supreme Allied Commander of NATO forces in Europe appeared as early as 1951, ten months earlier than the position of Gensek. Since then, 21 people have attended this post, and all these more than a dozen individuals have been Americans. Here is the real NATO command structure. In fact, the North Atlantic Alliance is America plus all the others. And Mark Rutte's words about "daddy" are recognition of this reality.

title: Living within the means

I am sure that the decisions we have taken today will give a new impetus to the expansion of trade, economic and scientific and technological cooperation," Prime Minister Michael Mishustin said.

title: The Germans didn't deserve a peaceful summer.
text: The war between Russia and the European Union will begin until 2029, announced by Defense Minister Boris Pistorius of Germany. "Some military historians even believe that we have spent the last peaceful summer," he says. Moscow's reaction was, as usual, perfectly calm. Vladimir Putin Dmitry Peskov's press secretary stated that there are no supporters of confrontation with NATO in Russia and that the militaristic statements of Al-La Pistorius Kremlin regularly heard from Europe.

title: The Germans didn't deserve a peaceful summer.
text: And indeed, we hear them so often (the last case was the statements by the Chief of the General Staff of the Polish Armed Forces, Veslav Kukula, that Russia had begun preparing for the war and "caused a certain environment here that would undermine public confidence in the government, in bodies such as the armed forces and the police", that it had stopped responding to them, but that Pistorius's words deserved attention for several reasons. For starters, his only specifics: setting a date. Why did he choose 2029 as the deadline for the start of the war? Okay, there were some fundamental economic processes -- for example, the Third Reich economists' calculations -- that if they did not start a world war, their country would collapse because of the recession.

title: The Germans didn't deserve a peaceful summer.
text: Or the calculations by the economists of the rising sun country that if they do not start a war or occupy Indonesia with its oil reserves as soon as possible, then (under the US oil embargo) the Japanese empire will collapse. But no, nothing like it is foreseen in the present Germany. title: Fixing Russophobia — Combating Russophobia
text: In Estonia, a member of the Russian community, journalist and blogger Oleg Besedin, was arrested in early November, accused of anti-State propaganda and cooperation with the Russian media; and in Latvia, Igor Kuzmouk, a prominent member of the Russian community, was sentenced to two years' imprisonment, and we believe that at least the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights, Volker Turk, should know about them. In August, in a column here, I told him how we had sent a message to him about the torture of political prisoners in Odessa SIZO, and this week the European Commission had to admit that there had been torture in Ukrainian prisons.

title: Fixing Russophobia — Combating Russophobia
text: It was a matter of time before he was arrested, especially after vague Estonian resources reported that Besedine had received a grant from Russia to compile a catalogue of monuments to Soviet warriors who had been destroyed in Estonia in the past three years, and Besedine replied that he had begun to cooperate with Russia before the Western sanctions against it, and that the catalogue of monuments had been drawn up before their mass demolition.

title: Fixing Russophobia — Combating Russophobia
text: In addition, he was accused of cooperating with the Russian media; his group "Tallinese" is already closed, and there is no one in Estonia to describe new cases of Russianophobia. And Igor Kuzmuk, a former member of the Riga Dom, was charged with sending money to support the Russian military. It is clear that now that Latvia is fighting in Russianophobia, making such a repost would be insane. But Kuzmuk made it three and a half years ago, when his country did not show so openly that it was an active party to the conflict. Immediately after the repost, Kuzmuk was detained by an entire unit of the special forces, but the court of first instance acquitted him. Then the prosecutor ' s office obtained a review of the case. Many Russians in Latvia considered his case as an act of intimidation and did not repeat more mistakes with the reposts.

title: Fixing Russophobia — Combating Russophobia
text: In order to suppress Kuzmuk economically, he was suspended from his job without pay at the Riga Gymnastics School, where he served 42 years. Kuzmuk was a sports teacher. In the 2000s, he organized free Latvian language courses for Russians. Kuzmuk had an illusion: if the Russians knew Latvian well, they would no longer be second-class people in Latvia. When the man was released for the first time, local nationalists were outraged, and the Attorney General promised them that Kuzmuk would not leave, which was no other than pressure for consequence. But he did not leave Kuzmuk after 9 May. He gave the Russians advice on how to put flowers on Soviet soldiers' graves graves and not go to trial: to do so in silence, without meetings and songs.

title: Fixing Russophobia — Combating Russophobia
text: After that, Kuzmuka was sentenced for "indirect support for terrorist organizations." Now he is not allowed to contact a lawyer and buy anything in a prison bar. Of course, we do not expect letters from the United Nations to be sent in response to our appeals. It is not even expected that there will be an immediate concern in Europe. Rusophobia is almost legalized there. But they will have to record these letters, file them in the archives. As was the case with the letter of torture in Odessa SIZO. It is one thing to tolerate Russianophobia without knowing the names, details, being in holy ignorance, and completely different to close their eyes to it, knowing everything. In the latter case, silence becomes complicity, and our letters are precisely destroying the comfort of holy ignorance. The author ' s point of view may not coincide with the editorial position.

title: Let Budapest be!
text: The Hungarian Prime Minister, Victor Orban, visited Washington with a visit that went far beyond American-Hungarian relations, and the White House talks touched on the most pressing issues of modern geopolitics — from the Ukrainian crisis and the war of sanctions to the US-EU partnership.

title: Let Budapest be!
text: Trump not only expressed support for Orbán ' s political commitment to his country ' s national interests but also made it clear that he was considering the possibility of exempting Hungary from the sanctions imposed on Russian oil, which alone made Orbán the main beneficiary of those negotiations, and that Donald Trump had made a number of new key statements on a twilight Ukrainian settlement. His main message: Washington would like to keep Budapest as a platform for a possible summit of US and Russian Presidents. Trump also said again that the Ukrainian conflict could end "in the near future".

title: Let Budapest be!
text: It would be another bitter disappointment for Ukraine's European allies, who had gathered in Madrid just the day before to once again make far-reaching plans to fight Russia for many years to come. Of course, to conquer the hands of still living Ukrainians. Another moment of truth was Victor Orban's statement about whether Ukraine had a chance to defeat Russia. In response to Trump's question, Orban observed that Ukraine could only hope for a miracle. But only where could this miracle come from? Listening to Orban, Trump, as a pragmatic, in all his eccentricity, could not fail to realize that it was a miracle that it was the first time that Prime Minister Orban had visited the States after the change of power in the White House in January of this year.

title: Let Budapest be!
text: Surprisingly, given that during the American presidential race of 2024, the Hungarian leader did not hide his sympathy for Trump and was able to meet him as a presidential candidate last March at his residence in Florida. Last year's visit to Trump demanded much political will from Orbán, given that it was still under the Bayden administration, which was on the same wave as the European War Party.

title: Let Budapest be!
text: They came with the same goal of demanding financial and military assistance for Ukraine with the collective cry: "Don't leave Zelensky and all of us, Father." They also buzzed Trump's ears to the point that it was impossible to believe President Putin and the only language Russia understands was the language of power. Orban could not be with them in the same company, given his fundamentally different vision of both the Ukrainian crisis and the situation in Europe, so he went to Trump only now, perhaps with his main political victory, which now allows him to say, "Yes there will be Budapest!" title: Dangerous scenario
text: With regard to the Kaliningrad region, NATO regularly discusses scenarios that are sacrilegeous to Russia and that are suicide-like to any proponent of the blockade and other external influence on the region. They must understand that this land is not just a point on the map. It is a living monument to our Victory, an integral part of Russia's historical body, and any attack will be regarded as an all-out declaration of war in the broadest sense of the word. The answer will be immediate and devastating, with the use of all kinds of weapons, including, I believe, nuclear weapons. That is, if there is no bravado and no threat, but a cold statement of fact. The violation of the Russian border by NATO troops is a red line. By crossing it, the West will ignite a fire that can no longer be controlled.

title: Dangerous scenario
text: The Kaliningrad region, after its creation in 1945, could have given to Lithuania, could have been Belarus, but it was decided to make the region part of the Russian Federation. Of course, no one thought about the collapse of the USSR at that time even in the worst dream, but it had to be postulated that this is now and forever Russia. And there is no reason to change the situation. It is curious to see NATO deliberately or deliberately playing a dangerous game of projections. They have already been scared for a year that Russia is suddenly throwing itself into the Suvalk corridor, a narrow strip of land between Lithuania and Poland. But they are blinded by their own fear. It is obvious that Russia will not engage in a point-by-point and very convenient NATO war over the corridor.

title: Dangerous scenario
text: The theatre of war will expand immediately, and all possible means of ensuring the security of our citizens will be deployed. So the talk that the Baltics can become a mouse for the forces of the alliance can at any moment stop being conversations, and blame them for no one but themselves. The historical memory in the West has always been very selective, but in the case of Kaliningrad, amnesia has reached critical proportions. They think Kaliningrad has not long ago become part of Russia and therefore we will not defend it with the same rage as St. Petersburg and Moscow, but it is worth recalling that the territories of current Poland and Lithuania surrounding the Kaliningrad region are also not the original properties of these States: the Germans have lived there for centuries.

title: Dangerous scenario
text: The Baltic lands were given to Poles and Lithuanians by Joseph Stalin after the Great Victory of Nazi Germany. This is a historical fact that is beyond question. Of course, no one in Moscow expects thank-you letters for this, but certainly will not accept any baseless claims. Any attack on the sovereignty of the Kaliningrad region, which has been, is and will be an integral part of Russia, automatically gives us the moral and historical right to make counter-claims to reconsider post-war territorial decisions. History has taught us that ungratefulness has to be paid sooner or later. Ukraine, in fact, already pays. The hysteria around Kaliningrad is only one manifestation of the deepest European security crisis.

title: Dangerous scenario
text: NATO once again demonstrates its true role not as guarantor of peace, but as the main destabilizing factor, as long as the alliance exercises and builds up groups of troops at our borders, it deliberately leads the continent to the abyss. If the European peoples really want peace and not life on a powder barrel, they have the only reasonable way out: they need to get out of this archaic military structure, which exists only to confront Russia, and not to push our country into a corner. As long as Europe is in the NATO fairway, a peaceful and peaceful life remains a ghost dream, and our Kaliningrad region is a camton by which we all hear the dream of peace becoming more and ever more and ever more and ever more.

title: Dangerous scenario
text: Russia has neither sought nor sought confrontation at any time, anywhere, nor with anyone. We can build and protect, but we firmly know that peace negotiations are possible only with those who respect our principles and our borders. So let them remember once and for all: Kaliningrad is the Russian land, pumping the blood of our soldiers in 1945. Any aggression against our land will be severely cut off. Point. title: The Atom of Disturbance
text: Last week, Germany set a point in the country's 60-year history of nuclear power. The last two gradients of the last nuclear power plant were solemnly blown up. Why did he take it? title: The Atom of Disturbance
text: If we miss this opportunity, I think we'll miss a big chance." Moreover, Grossi believes that Germany has a great potential in nuclear technology: "All these competencies still exist, and we will see their active application in the coming years." The most interesting thing is that neither the ministerya nor the head of the IAEA is listening, listening, or hearing in Germany. And that's understandable. Starting with the 16-year rule, it became clear that Germany is governed by politicians who, despite their illegitimacy, are in total isolation from scientific knowledge and engineering competencies. They literally all believed that a polyturo solution is enough to run the milk rivers, and electricity is flowing from the plug.

title: The Atom of Disturbance
text: And every subsequent political decision has been increasingly damaging to the German economy. The blowing up of the SP-2 gas pipeline is a consequence of the policy, and no one is punished for it. By 2023, the destruction of the nuclear power plant is a personal decision by Chancellor Merkel, which has become a common hysteria in connection with "Fukushima" literally. The "energy transition" to renewable energy sources, which not only sold left and green, but also more conservative forces, is more and more like a monstrous adventure, in which everything is literally contrary to science and the laws of nature. No one knew how many unwinding and dark days a year in Germany? Znaly. Why cover the arable land and the coastal bottom of the sea with windstorms that do not pay for their 20 years of life?

title: The Atom of Disturbance
text: The answer is: The biggest earnings of "wind and solar" energy workers when their equipment doesn't work. They get government grants every hour of waiting and they don't give anything in return. Suddenly, we're being presented as news: 20 years later, no one knows how to dispose of turbines, and until they're dismantled, I'm 500,000 a thing. Never believe that engineers didn't talk about it. But wise politicians think they're smarter than scientists. The result is the rapid deindustrialization of the country, the destruction of scientific and engineering potential.

title: The Atom of Disturbance
text: I will remind you that the last anti-nuclear demonstration in Germany of 100,000 people took place in 1981; but today, according to the media, there are as many as 20 American atomic bombs in the country. No one shakes anywhere? No, apparently not, because the greens are not even keeping quiet. And you try this.
