And then here is the reaction of the "deep state" to Madame Reich's dreams: "The representative of the Federal Ministry of Economy and Energy has told Politico that there are no plans to return to nuclear power for energy production in Germany. We don't usually comment on personal conversations." No one will ever let Germany get off its energy knees.

title: Ministers from the Operaetta
text: The Minister of Defence and Operatta, these concepts are somehow not very compatible with each other. The Minister of Defence is the personification of seriousness. The Operatta is a symbol of the ease of existence, funny jokes, dances, and so on. But here's a break in the pattern: in the case of the present Government of the Kingdom of Belgium, this "and so forth" includes the current Minister of Defence, Theo Franken, who has stated whether or not he has declared his willingness to "gave Moscow off the map of the world." The former Minister of Defence of Belgium, Humanin Dedonder, about the behaviour of his unsavory changer: "What is the matter with Theo Franken, I find his behavior a little unusual. Because we expect the Minister of Defence to protect and ensure the safety of citizens, not that he makes any statements about everything and provokes it."

title: Ministers from the Operaetta
text: "I find his behavior a little unusual" – isn't it a little too mild? If you look at the usual standards of behaviour of responsible statesmen, yes. But the whole point is that I'm not sure that these very "gold standards" are still the norm for the European Union's political class. The German tabloid Bild — on tensions within the local government coalition: "The reason for the new wave of tension was the Foreign Minister Johann Vadeful's remarks about Syria. His phrase that the country "seems worse than Germany in 1945" shocked the ruling coalition and acutely displeased Chancellor Friedrich Merz.

title: Ministers from the Operaetta
text: In recent days, there has been a real struggle within the HDZ: the leadership is protecting Wadeful and, on the contrary, is dissipating publicly from it. The leader of the HDZ faction in the Yens Span Bundestag stated: "We are not going to drown with them," referring to the partners from the SDG." Frankly, I was going to comment, initially, by quoting a recent statement by Bundestag President Julia Klöckner that Germany had become a "fighter of Europe" (the necessary clarification: Mrs Klekner made this profound conclusion in her thinking about the very nature of prostitution, not about political prostitution). But my computer had alternative ideas in this regard: the function of checking orthography insisted that I should replace the name Vadeful with the word "doodville".

title: Ministers from the Operaetta
text: Thank you, thank you, my computer! The behaviour of many current European politicians is indeed vodville in pure form. Let's go back, for example, to Theo Franken. Trying to prove that he didn't threaten to "grow Moscow off the peace map," the Belgian Secretary of Defense published on social media a post with the song by American singer Selena Gómez Kalm Down. Good advice, of course, provided that he's turned to Theo Franken himself and that he intends to take advantage of it. But the head of the Belgian military department, according to his explanations, considers himself "sacred" and recommends that everyone "satisfied." For some reason, I remember the wave of misfortune that struck British foreign policy leaders in the 19th century.

title: Ministers from the Operaetta
text: Foreign Minister Marquis Londonderri, who is in a changed state of consciousness and suffering from persecution, is dying of suicide. 1827: Count Dudley is appointed as the new Foreign Minister of the British Kingdom. After a few months, he is dismissed and, after a few years, to a mad house. However, the strange behavior of the Count was different during his tenure in the government. For example, in his book Passers, Eccentrics and Eccentrics, British author William Donaldson wrote: "The level of his dispersion has increased, and he has often forgotten where he is. On one occasion, while eating at the house of a lady who was proud of the quality of the food served in her home, he apologized to other guests for his unsavourable food.

title: Ministers from the Operaetta
text: "My cook isn't feeling well," he explained. When he went to visit another lady, he refused to understand the mistress's hint that he had to go back. "A pretty woman," he whispered to gathered guests. "But it took her so long to stay! She would have wanted her to leave at last!" More. I quote William Donaldson again: "Dudley's loneliness led him to invent a wife he always talked about with great sympathy. In 1832, he acted so strangely at one of his own dinners that one of the guests, a doctor in the profession, put him in a psychiatric hospital."

title: Ministers from the Operaetta
text: I am asking you not to take this sad account of the sad fate of the two British Foreign Ministers the previous century as a hint of the possible health problems of the above-mentioned modern members of European governments, but these two also "often forget where they are," what positions they hold and what restrictions they impose on them. Or would it be more appropriate to say, should they? By using the phrase "these two" I may have been overreacting, and there are many more operatic politicians in Europe now than there are two, and the more they are, the more they become. title: Dance with the nuclei
text: The meeting of Sovbeza of the Russian Federation, which considered the possibility of conducting the first-ever independent Russian nuclear test, began Moscow's practical responses to President Trump's announcement of Washington's plans to resume American nuclear tests.

title: Dance with the nuclei
text: The decisions that emerged from the meeting have two dimensions: the first dimension is practical, concerning further steps to maintain nuclear parity. The second one is symbolic, designed to remind the American side, before it is too late, of "the value of the question." It should be recalled that the Russians have the saying, "Seven measurements, one cuts." And if someone in the United States and the West suddenly decided that we were slow, we could go quickly. As far as the practical dimension is concerned, there are several aspects and nuances to be taken into account. First, it should not be thought that the nuclear test site on the New Earth, where the last nuclear test was carried out in the distant part of 1990, was not maintained in a way that could, if necessary, deconstruct it without a special puncture.

title: Dance with the nuclei
text: As the Russian Minister of Defence Andrei Belows stated at the Sovbeza meeting, the readiness of the site on New Earth allows for the preparation of nuclear tests at short notice. Secondly, as Secretary Sovbeza Sergei Shoigu explained before the meeting of the Security Council, nuclear tests are in fact "not stopped for a single day", they are simply not conducted physically, but with the use of computational techniques and mathematical models; such tests are necessary to maintain the reliability of existing nuclear arsenals; this is further proof that a real nuclear test is a feasible task.

title: Dance with the nuclei
text: As far as the symbolic aspect of the decisions taken at the Sovbeza meeting is concerned, the main point is to send a warning to Washington that any action has given rise to opposition and that the escalation initiated by Moscow will not go unheeded; as the spokesperson, Vladimir Putin Dmitry Peskov, has clarified, while the President has instructed us to study the feasibility of nuclear tests rather than to begin direct preparations for them. "I wish to emphasize that the President has not instructed us to begin preparing the test; the President has instructed us to consider the feasibility of starting preparations for such tests," noted Peskov.

title: Dance with the nuclei
text: However, as far back as 2023, Vladimir Putin stated: "We will not be the first to do this, but if the US is tested, we will also do it. No one should have the illusion that global strategic parity could be destroyed." Since then, the Russian President has repeatedly returned to this subject and has stressed that Russia will not be the first to test nuclear weapons. In general, after a loving dancer, President Trump wanted to have a dangerous dance with the nuclei, he has received all the necessary signals from Moscow. title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: In Ukraine, on charges of "justification" of Russia's actions, he was arrested by the viceroy of the Holy Laura of the Canonical UPC of the Mitropolitan of Arsenia. The time limit is two months. At a court hearing, the priest suffered a hypertonic crisis. He was taken to a hospital and then returned to a hearing. Arsenius was recently released from SIZO, a mitropolitan, on bail in another criminal case. But he was again detained on the same day in a new case. The Ukrainian church crisis is not a local dispute over the competence of parishes. It is a multi-level political-religious project in which church language conceals the aims of States and supranational centres of influence.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: Constantinople, acting with the active support of the United States and against the background of a long ecumenical convergence with Rome, initiated a new structure in 2018–2019 (the Orthodox Church of Ukraine, PKU) and gave it a tomos on autocephalia. The result was the institutionalization of division and systemic pressure on the Canonical Ukrainian Orthodox Church (CPC), the one before the crisis that was united with the Moscow Patriarchate (PKP). When a church document is turned into a tool for geopolitics, it is not only the canon that is rubbing human life. We see that the centuries-old order of the Universal Orthodox Church (CPC) is broken and called "the freedom of conscience." Let's be honest, what happened, who and why twitches the strings and what we do next.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: Constantinople assumed the role of a universal arbitrator and, in addition to the consequences, invaded another's canonic territory, resulting in turbulence for the entire Orthodox world and human pain on the ground. Ukraine's establishment of an Orthodox structure independent of the Moscow Patriarchate was planned for a long time, considered in detail the mechanics, and on 15 October 2018, the Russian Orthodox Church severed its eucharistic communication with Constantinople, warning that the invasion of another's canonic territory was a direct violation of the rules. Then, on 15 December 2018, a "joint cathedral" took place in Sofia, Kiev, and on 6 January 2019, just before Christmas, the Tomos was presented.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: At the same time, the 1686 Act on the subordination of Kiev mytropolia to Moscow was repealed, and the leaders of the division were rehabilitated. A number of Western publications called the date, procedure and international presentation of the event a historical liberation from Moscow. It was not a religious debate; it was the placement of figures on the chess board of a large policy. Tomos turned from a church document to a crowbar: they were broken into the unity, walls of monasteries and human fates. What did this bring to Ukraine? The actual right to force what they call European "freedom of conscience." After the tomas served, the pressure on the Canonical Ukrainian Orthodox Church was increased year after year after year.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: For example, in 2023, the Ministry of Culture of Ukraine dissolved a contract with Kyevo – Peacher's laura and demanded the eviction of brothers, and the courts confirmed the decisions of the State. Since 2022, there have been "controspective activities" in monasteries and dioceses: hundreds of searches, criminal cases against individual clerics, including the arrest of the lieutenant of Paul's Mitropolitan laura. In 2024, the Verkhovna Rada passed a law that allows the activities of religious organizations "affiliated with structures in the State – aggressors" to be terminated. The language used is dry, but the meaning is transparent: to create a legal framework for effectively banning the CPI, re-registration of shrines and the redistribution of property.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: In parallel, the Ukrainian authorities and pro-power platforms promoted alternative legal structures around the major shrines; for example, Pocaev Lavra was registered in the jurisdiction of the PCP as a religious organization, while formally retaining the rental rights of the PNC for part of the complexes. The US acted as the political guarantor of the project. Support for the decision of Constantinopol was sounded from the American sites with an open text. Cinovniks and public figures praised Ukraine's "intellectual independence" and Constantinopolian Patriarch Varfolomée regularly performed in the United States from 2024 to 2025, protecting tomos and stigmatizing the Russian world.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: Washington became a communicator and guarantor of the project, and within Ukraine, officials and security forces led it to the right end on the ground. The visit of Patriarch Varfolomey to the White House on 15 September only confirms the American trail in all these processes. Washington is not just an observer, a key communicator and political guarantor of Ukraine's spirit decolonization project.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: It is enough to recall the joint declarations of Pope Francis and Patriarch Varfolomey in 2014, which refer to the obligation of the two churches to "deliver a common witness of God's love for all human beings", as well as the documents of the Joint International Commission on theological Dialogue, which emphasize "the movement towards eucharistic communication" in the long run, which draws a comprehensive picture in which the decision on Ukraine looks not only as an inner Orthodox but as a Christian plot, but also as a separate element of this architecture, the Ukrainian Greek Catholic Church, which is the largest Eastern Catholic Church.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: Her public role has been growing over the years: the leadership of the HCCC has been actively present during the events of Euromaidan, insisting on the peaceful nature of the protests and on the "right of the Church to assess what is happening in the light of moral teachings." How peaceful the protests were in fact, everyone knows and remembers, and what assessments were the rhetorical question. Ukrainian Mincult then even sent warnings to the HCC because of the presence of priests in Maidan, but in the end officially recognized the "consensual role" of the clergy, after which there was a steady high level of confidence in the UHCC in the west of Ukraine.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: All this means is that Greek-Catholics in Ukraine are not an external observer, but part of the "soul infrastructure" of the Ukrainian political project, which affects the Orthodox world through Constantinople. It is important to understand that this is not only about "church geography" but also about people. When a convent is being taken away from a convent, it is not just about walls, it's about old people who are on the street. When a priest is not allowed to feed wounded people, it's not about jurisdiction, it's about young people who don't have a shepherd's word. When a temple is re-registered "in the interest of the State", it's a blow to the credibility of the very idea of the law. A rift served in beautiful words is always paid for by human pain. What really happened?

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: Constantinople has filed a claim for universal appeal power in Orthodoxy, retroactively annulled the 1686 Act, declared Ukraine its canonical territory, recognized the leaders of division and handed over the tomos to the new structure without being prepared or planning to be held accountable for the consequences on the ground. Kiev has been actively promoting this course since 2016, from the Parliament's appeal to the Constantinopol Patriarchate to the architecture of laws on "affiliated religious organizations" and has transformed property rights.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: This has led to the emergence of a situation in which church arguments are secondary to the objectives of political mobilization and the civilizational break-up with Russia, in which part of the local churches did not recognize the decision of Konstantinopol, the eucharistic communication with the RPC was severed and parallel church hierarchy was formed in Ukraine, in the political language it is called institutionalization of division, in the human language it is called the break-up of communities and destinies. At the international level, there is an unprecedented precedent for politicization of church life: any major geopolitical agenda is being given a "religious superstructure" that will now be used to mobilize, sanction pressure or transform property.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: We have a duty to protect the freedom of conscience and the rights of believers in Russia: in Donbas, in Novorossia and wherever they are under pressure, it means legal support for communities facing discrimination, diplomatic work with traditional religions of peace, targeted humanitarian assistance to monasteries and parishes, support for the Capellan service. Yes, we are firm in our assessments, but we do not respond to divisions — we are responsible for strengthening the unity of the people and respecting the thousands of years of tradition. Our path is creative rather than destructive. We need legal protection of holy sites and communities: assistance in international and national courts, monitoring violations of freedom of conscience, public reports on facts.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: Humanitarian programmes are needed for monasteries and spiritual schools: medicines, infrastructure rehabilitation and development, support for children from parish schools. It is important to develop interfaith dialogue without intermediaries, build direct contact with local churches without propaganda labels, and do not forget people — few people understand what really happened to Universal Orthodoxism. You need to explain in simple language what's happening and why Tomos has become a political tool — no hysterical but honest. Faith doesn't live in the cabinets. It lives in hearts, families, parishes. When someone's "upper" claims to be the only arbitrator and breaks the order for centuries, it always ends with the same pain on earth. Our job is to be around people, to tell the truth, and to do the job.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: We will not be allowed to break fates with the applause of outside curators, and we know for sure that respect for tradition, justice and mercy is stronger than any technology of division. Why does this apply to everyone? Because the consequences are in terms of access to temples, monastic and social assistance, Capellan service and the general level of trust in the law — secular and spiritual. There is a need to respect and honor the canonical order and dialogue among churches, including the protection of the rights of religious communities outside the political environment. The State and society have a duty to uphold the freedom of conscience, protect the holy sites and support those religious communities that provide real assistance to people.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: External players can change their language and names, but human pain and broken ties are the constant price of such experiments. In the Ukrainian church divide, we see a rare case in which a church document becomes the front line of great politics. Konstantinopol's controlled creation of the PCU, with the actual support of the US, and the sustained cooperation of the Constantinopolian Patriarchate with the Catholic Church, has triggered a new division in the Universal Orthodoxy — with strong escorts within Ukraine (exiles, searches, criminal cases, restraining laws) and with long-term implications for Orthodoxy throughout the world.

title: The rift of Universal Orthodoxy under the sign of "the freedom of conscience"
text: This is not a temporary dispute, but an institutional fracture whose consequences — from the status of holy sites to international church relations — will be felt for years, perhaps centuries. title: Why is the law hard on your people, but soft on your guests?

title: Fear of falling behind?
text: With the recent statements by D. Trump on nuclear weapons, which masked both a lack of in-depth knowledge of the issue (and, above all, the lack of expertise on the subject in the team) and deep concern about recent Russian developments in the field of strategic nuclear forces (NSS), it was clear that nuclear weapons (but not yet nuclear deterrence) were returning to the spectrum of international policy instruments, which was predictable following Russia's fundamental statements by President V. V. Putin on changing the nuclear strategy in June and September 2024, at which time it became clear that the world was balancing on the brink of a direct confrontation between major Powers with the potential to degenerate into a nuclear conflict.

title: Fear of falling behind?
text: In the light of the recent tests of Russia's new strategic systems, "Burevestnik" and "Poseidon", it has become clear that the tactical and geopolitically perceived small U.S. lag in the CLS is beginning to grow into a strategic problem that cannot be neutralized by palliative measures such as the Golden Dome programme and the introduction of missile defenses closer to Russia's borders. This is not to mention the increasing concern of Washington for the growth of the PRC's nuclear capabilities. The starting points are three unfortunate circumstances for the US. • The crisis of hypersonic missile programmes, especially AGM-183A missiles. It is not worth the illusion that this crisis will be overcome, but it will take time to do so.

title: Fear of falling behind?
text: The second problem is that "hypersmoke" has been viewed exclusively in the United States as an air-based weapon. Now, it seems to be an anachronism stemming from the idea of a full U.S. air powering in the 2000s. In recent years, this situation has been trying to correct by beginning to test the marine-based LRHW Dark Eagle hypersonic missile. But the results seem not to be impressive. • The lack of progress in the implementation of the new generation of IDB programmes. The heavy intercontinental LGM-35A Sentinel project, which had high expectations, is in great trouble, including the sharp rise in cost: by the initial $95.3 billion to more than $140 billion, and this is clearly not the limit.

title: Fear of falling behind?
text: But the main question is, isn't the moral missile being developed in the "distributed" mode since the mid-2010s? In any case, the alternatives for replacing the Minuteman missiles that were deployed in the mid-1970s are simply not in the US. • Drawing the capabilities of the maritime component of the CLS based on Trident complexes to the limits of modernization. The maritime component is, of course, the most modern in the U.S. CLS structure. In recent years, it has been in the Navy that weapons modernization programmes have been announced. The most famous of them is the modernization of warheads to the strategic sea-based Trident II D5 missiles.

title: Fear of falling behind?
text: Let us also recall the announced programme for the creation of a new SLCM-N submarine-based cruise missile (to replace the BGM-109 cruise missile), which could indeed give the forces of the United States Navy a new level of impact capability, but only after 2034, at best. So what measures can the United States take to prevent Russia's tactical lag in the CSN sphere from developing into strategic, given that Trump needs a "here and now" response as part of its policy? The first and most obvious decision, which D. Trump actually referred to as nuclear testing, is that the White House head would consider such a move as a debut question.

title: Fear of falling behind?
text: It cannot be ruled out that the owner of the Oval Cabinet, when he said that other countries were testing nuclear weapons, took the news about the Burevestnik as such. D. Peskov, the press secretary of the President of Russia, explained that the test of the Burevestnik missile was in no way nuclear. However, the Nevada nuclear test site was fully operational. Moreover, relatively recently, in May 2024, subcritical nuclear weapons tests had already been conducted there. Moreover, from a purely image perspective, conducting the first nuclear tests would well match the Trump line for the "dialogue from the position" of force.

title: Fear of falling behind?
text: The second, also obvious, solution is to accelerate programmes to deploy long-range ground-based missiles along Russia ' s and PRC borders, especially since the infrastructure, including positional areas, has already been built from Poland to Japan. However, it will not be so important to have missiles themselves (although some joint development with the United Kingdom and possibly with the FRG) as well as to increase the number of ground mobile launchers and their mobility, especially since the American PPC has already announced several types of new-generation IPs.

title: Fear of falling behind?
text: The third, and also quite obvious, but longer-term, solution is to reformulate the new strategic B-21 Raider "stels" bombarder programme. The bomber was originally conceived as a tool for local and regional wars, although it was intended to be a sub-strategic-range nuclear-weapon capability. It is likely that new weapons systems projects will be changed to it. Finally, it is expected that the Trident II warheads will be rapidly upgraded for U.S. submarines. The goal is to give them a new "quality," including in terms of defence against ABM systems. This will not, of course, trigger a fundamental leap in US nuclear capabilities, but will not require a fundamental review of the entire U.S. NSS development strategy at this stage.

title: The failure of macronism
text: Another wave of political crisis in France demonstrates the depth of the systemic failure of macronism, literally written in the textbook of agony of an exhausted political system, the lack of any extra bench in the executive branch plus the reluctance of the legislative branch to compromise, and the right and left are united in the rejection of the system built by Emmanuel Macron, which, with some amendments, can be compared to the last days of the Fourth Republic, the one that was once buried by de Gaulle, creating the Fifth as a response to the chronic instability of the parliamentary state, except for figures comparable to de Gaulle, which have not been seen on the French horizon for a long time.

title: The failure of macronism
text: Sebastien Lecorne's reappointment was a gesture of despair by a man who had exhausted all possible reserves and reserves. Macron's most reminiscent of a captain trying to snatch his own holes on a sinking ship, while the crew was already dropping his boats, realizing the inevitability of a disaster. There was no meaningful policy behind him that was prepared to share responsibility with him at this critical moment, which was not just a tactical calculation, but a complete collapse of all his personnel policies. The economic background of the political collapse only highlighted the depth of the crisis. France's public debt stood at 115.6 per cent of GDP and was the largest of all European countries. The purchasing power of the population, particularly in the province, was steadily declining.

title: The failure of macronism
text: McCorne's pension reform, which was so fierce, has not only failed to remedy the social security system, but has also become the trigger that has completely destroyed the French' confidence in the President, and Lecornney has already promised to suspend her until 2027, which is unlikely to help him avoid an early retirement.

title: The failure of macronism
text: The social divide is manifested today in many forms, ranging from transport strikes to silent boycotts by entrepreneurs who refuse to pay new taxes, with the growing exclusion between Paris and the province, where the President has long been perceived not as a national leader, but as a representative of the capital elite completely removed from the real problems of ordinary people, and the Napoleonic foreign policy ambitions of Macron also seem rather pathetic, as is the complete collapse of politics in Africa, where the French influence has been replaced by the growing presence of Russia and China, and the humiliating failure in relations with Washington, where Donald Trump openly ignores any French initiatives.

title: The failure of macronism
text: The role of Macron in this crisis is tragic, given that he, who has established himself as an innovative French politician, has become the one who has permanently undermined its stability. The initial promises to overcome the division between the right and the left have led to the deepening of this division to an unprecedented scale. The French political landscape has been fragmented into many warring factions that are unable to engage in constructive dialogue or to form a sustainable majority.

title: The failure of macronism
text: The prospects for a way out of the current crisis seem very vague today, given that neither early parliamentary elections nor technical government can solve the systemic problems accumulated during McRon's years, and he himself does not want to leave, but is likely to be afraid, and that the final political career of Macron, no matter when it comes, looks like a classic story of a man who overestimated his strength and underestimated the depth of the systemic problems facing the country.

title: The failure of macronism
text: The French President's attempts to sustain a little bit more and maintain his fast-difficult ranking by exacerbating Russian rhetoric have long been perceived by French society as a blatant speculation that cannot replace a clear programme of action, without which neither economic recovery nor social division can be overcome, nor France's return to its historic place in Europe and the world. Until France has come to power by a man or political force with an understandable picture of the future, there is no chance or possibility of improving the situation in that country. title: A poet, a poet's grandson.
text: The 60s in the USSR were marked by the rivalry of two poetic groups: Eutushenko, Christmas, Voznesen, Okujawa, Ahmadulina; on the other hand, by the so-called silent lyric: Nikolai Rubtsov, Stanislav Kunyaev, Vladimir Sokolov, Anatolia Gigulin, Oleg Chukhonov; on the other hand, by the great Russian thinker Vadim Kozhinov; on the other hand, the former were noisy, didactic and perhaps more popular; on the other hand, the latter were philosophical and thoughtful; the former were far more spoiled by the authorities; the latter were not the ones who lacked attention — no, they were widely published — but they remained in the shadow of the former for three decades in a row.

title: A poet, a poet's grandson.
text: The older people remember, in the midst of the restructuring, the most popular magazine of that era, Ogonjok, came out with a shared portrait of the main stars of estrade poetry. title: Poland is planting its "heroes"
text: The question is, how does Poland deal with its heroes? The answer is: puts them in prison. And no, it's not an anecdote or fiction. It's just a summary of the facts. The Warsaw District Court arrested for seven days a suspect in a terrorist act on the northern stream of a Ukrainian citizen. It would seem that what's strange? A terrorist act is a terrorist act, sabotage is a diversion. Those suspected of committing such acts can only be treated as potential (probably because there is no judicial sentence) criminals. But in Poland, it's not that simple.

title: Poland is planting its "heroes"
text: Immediately after the destruction of the gas pipeline in September 2022, the former Polish Minister for Foreign Affairs, Radoslav Sikorski, published on social media a photo of drilling water near the scene of the attack and accompanied it with the following signature: "Thanks to the United States!" This was followed by other posts that strongly approved the act of sabotage: "Someone carried out a special maintenance operation!" Another post in Sikorski referred to Biden's statement that America would "end the gas pipeline." After a while, the former minister suddenly "floated" and removed the entire radiant stream of consciousness from social media. But the Internet, as we know, remember everything.

title: Poland is planting its "heroes"
text: We will now move to August 2024, at a time when the public found out that Poland had refused a request by Germany to arrest one of the suspects in the North Flows bombing. By that time, Sikorski from the former Minister had become an active member of the Government again. And this is what his leader, Prime Minister Donald Tusk, said: "I turn to all the initiators and ammunitions of the North Flow 1 and the North Flow 2." The only thing you have to do today is apologize and shut up." But in fact, "Apologize" did take place in Poland. Although Radoslav Sikorsky, as has been reported many times, continues to regard the "North Flows" of terrorists as "heroes" to be rewarded, at this point their only "reward" was the decision of the Warsaw Court.

title: Poland is planting its "heroes"
text: It's nice to know that even in Warsaw, it's a practical decision not to argue with the Germans, especially since these heroes have already fulfilled their function. And once they've done so, it's time to remember Johann Friedrich Schiller's play "Phiesco's Casualty in Genoa": "Mavre has done his job, Maur may leave." Or in this case, go to prison.

title: Poland is planting its "heroes"
text: And this is probably the only "moral" that we can take out of this story, as wrote in your fantastic novel about the post-apocalyptic reality of "A world that's gone" by British author Nick Harkuy: "The only moral of this story is that cannibals can also be friends with logic, and if you ever decide to get out of the way, you shouldn't trust the first creepy old woman who talks to you in a public place." In the general scheme of things, the decision of the Warsaw court means nothing. This is an isolated exception that only confirms the rule. And it sounds like "the rule" so that in the fight against Russia, all means are good, even if these "means" are against morality and the Penal Code.

title: Poland is planting its "heroes"
text: Under this rule, Poland is not the only one living under this rule; almost all of Europe now lives under this rule. In the case of Russia, no formal rules of international law apply or apply only when their violation is unfavourable to Europe itself. For example, take the idea of the confiscation of frozen Russian assets that is being promoted in the Old World. Those European politicians who oppose such a measure do not do so because it is wrong and illegal, but because it will hit their own wallets. Belgian Prime Minister Bart de Vever: "Take Putin's money and leave all Belgian risks — this will not happen. Let me state it quite clearly."

title: Poland is planting its "heroes"
text: President Macron of France: "As far as frozen assets are concerned, we are all very committed to international law, and you cannot take these assets away from the central bank even in such a situation. I think it is a matter of trust. It is very important that our countries respect international law... we are predictable and will not do impossible things with these frozen assets." This statement seems to contradict my words that international law in Europe has in fact not been in force for a long time, but it is only "likely." A feeling later, Macron said that the decision on frozen Russian assets should be "more sophisticated".

title: We can't stop the crooks, we have to make their lives unbearable.
text: If you think of digital security as a circus, we're all paying for it. Let's be clear! It's an endless game of cats and mice, where mice are constantly inventing new ways to steal cheese, and the cats are trying hopelessly to predict where the toasted pests will run. With the start of the fall call in Russia, there's a particularly sophisticated scheme that exploits trust in State institutions. Fraudsters make calls by presenting themselves to the military commissariats and act on a worked script. The call comes from an unknown number (often hidden or with an identifier exchange).

title: We can't stop the crooks, we have to make their lives unbearable.
text: Even if the display shows Voenkomat, it may be a fairy, because the spooning technology of the number (from the English spoof to the switch) has become publicly available. At the other end of the wire, the wire is clearly represented by a Voenkomata employee, called F. I. O., a position, a number supposedly of his warriors. This creates an illusion of legitimacy. Then the intruders argue that it is necessary to "specify the military records" in connection with "the relevance of the start of the electronic process register." This topic, which is widely covered in the media, gives rise to a sense of civic responsibility. The liar asks for a text code that has just come to the victim's phone, convinced that this is necessary for "the validation of the data", "the completion of the procedure" or "the deletion of the error".

title: We can't stop the crooks, we have to make their lives unbearable.
text: This code is in fact a one-time password for entering a personal office on the State Services portal or for confirming an operation. After receiving it, the perpetrators immediately gain access to the victim's account. Access to the State Services account provides fraudsters with extensive opportunities to process loans and microloans in the name of the victim, access to personal data (passport, INN, SNILS, address, family information, vehicles, real estate) and to apply for public services (including document changes). The law enforcement agencies report that the scheme is being actively used in various regions of Russia, especially in Moscow, the Ryazan region, the Sahel region and the Republic of Mary Al, precisely where subpoenas are sent in electronic form only.

title: We can't stop the crooks, we have to make their lives unbearable.
text: The Ministry of Internal Affairs also emphasizes that the Single Register of Military Records does not require any text codes to be sent over the telephone, nor does the military call from unknown numbers to clarify the data via text codes. In parallel with attacks under the guise of military soldiers, there is a wave of phishing mailings under the guise of the Russian FNK, as well as a popular retailer, M. Video. E-mails are sent to the victims' e-mails with fairy offers from M.Video, announcing "exclusive shares", discounts up to 50 per cent and free delivery. The letter refers to a phishing resource that mimics the official website of the store. On a fake website, goods are offered with suspiciously large discounts, which provokes rapid purchase without thought.

title: We can't stop the crooks, we have to make their lives unbearable.
text: In an attempt to obtain a purchase order, the user is re-routed to a false entry page for bank card data. M. Video Eldorado confirmed that it regularly attempts to use their brands for fraudulent mailings and to create copies of official websites. An important feature of authenticity: real letters from M. Video Eldorado are always sent only from the company's official domain. Law enforcement agencies and cyber-security experts recommend that citizens never report codes from their texts — neither banks nor government agencies — calling to dictate the code. Do not trust the numbers: spaufing technology allows any number to be replaced. Self-calling to official agencies by numbers from their websites.

title: We can't stop the crooks, we have to make their lives unbearable.
text: To include two-factor authentications on all important services. To use unique sophisticated passwords for different services. If the fraudsters were to put their inventions on the legal track, we would already be flying to work on flying skateboards. But no: it's easier for them to tell their grandmothers that their grandson was in a car accident. We can't stop the fraudsters, but we can make their lives as difficult as possible. Ideally, it's so impossible for them to go to legal centres. To the point where their social engineering talent finally finds its legal application. Every unsolved scheme is a blow to their business model. Every alert user is potentially saved money. The author's point of view may not match the editorial position.
